label: Associate Professor of Architecture
summary: Associate Professor of Architecture at North Carolina Central University, founder of the Eyrie Initiative Community Design Center — the first community design center at an HBCU in North Carolina — and architect with research interests spanning community-engaged design, architectural pedagogy, the intersection of fine art printmaking and architectural image production, and digital humanities.

work.name: North Carolina Central University, Department of Art & Design
work.summary: Inaugural professor of architecture at NCCU, focusing on teaching, curriculum development, student recruitment, and building the first pre-architecture program at an HBCU in North Carolina.
highlights: Founder, Eyrie Initiative Community Design Center (2024–present) — first community design center at an HBCU in North Carolina
Coordinator, Pre-Architecture Concentration (2023–present) — first pre-architecture program at an HBCU in North Carolina
Developed B.Arch degree proposal for UNC System Board of Governors (2024–present)

work.name: Savannah College of Art and Design, Department of Architecture
work.summary: Teaching across all studio levels of the curriculum, with focused work in undergraduate and graduate architecture studios, architectural representation, graphics for the building arts, and beginning design.
highlights: Department Chair (2007–2012) — led nationally recognized NAAB-accredited M.Arch program enrolling 550+ students
Visiting Professor, University of Hawaii at Manoa (2007)

work.name: The Parallax View, WRUU 107.5FM
work.summary: Weekly program engaging listeners on the design and urbanism shaping civic life.

work.summary: Nationally syndicated television program; co-host and lead designer of episodes filmed in Savannah, GA.

work.summary: Boutique design practice providing critical solutions and strategies for diverse regional and international clients.

interests.name: Research Areas
keywords: Fine Art Printmaking & Architectural Image Production

projects.name: NCCU Eyrie Initiative Community Design Center
projects.summary: First community design center at an HBCU in North Carolina, directing community-engaged design research and practice. Current work includes the Fayetteville Street / Hayti Heritage Corridor Initiative (Preservation Durham, $10,000 contract) and 10 National Trust HOPE Crew student fellowships ($50,000 total).

projects.name: CLOUD Collaborative Research Project
projects.summary: Practice-based research initiative treating atmospheric clouds as a vehicle for exploring computational form, representation, and metaphor, using Python and Blender. Co-authorship of the 2025 cataloging framework and public contributor call.

projects.name: Digital Eagles Nest
projects.summary: Cross-disciplinary digital humanities project capturing and digitizing NCCU's historic campus through 3D scanning, photogrammetry, archival research, and virtual presentation — blending architectural, cultural, and social histories into a public digital campus resource.

projects.name: Fine Art Printmaking & Architectural Image Production
projects.summary: Research examining how lithography and fine art printmaking shaped the public reception of major American architectural projects — an underexplored intersection of architectural history and art history. NEH Award for Faculty at HBCUs submitted ($37,500, under review).
